rag_rules: Generate output guarantees using only visible feature names from the component type scope, with bounds derived from requirement thresholds.
Place guarantees in component type annexes when referring to input/output ports; use implementation annexes only for assign statements.
generic_rules: Use eq helpers to encapsulate boolean conditions for clarity and type safety.
Ensure all identifiers in guarantees are explicitly visible in the component scope or declared as constants within the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use guarantee "label": expression; syntax with logical operators (and, or, >=, <=) for range conditions.
Declare constants with int, real, or bool types; never use AADL property names as variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) directly in AGREE expressions.
Do not place guarantees in implementation annexes—use assign for implementation-level bindings instead.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
